name: Small Seed Space in PRNG
description: A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) uses a relatively small seed space, making it susceptible to brute force attacks. Since PRNGs are deterministic once seeded, an attacker who collects outputs can test all possible seeds until finding a match, then predict all subsequent outputs.
detection_pattern: TRANSFORM[prng_seeding] receives a seed value from a source with limited entropy (e.g., EXTERNAL[time], STRUCTURAL[pid], or a small constant range), then feeds into EGRESS or AUTH nodes. The seed space is insufficiently large, allowing brute-force enumeration of all possible seed values to predict PRNG output used in security-sensitive contexts.
